meaning: n.看门狗,监察人(员),监督人(员)adj.看门狗的,充当监察人员的
examples: Are you taking the place of the watchdog?
He barked as if he were a real watchdog: Bow, wow, wow!
The trap caught me and the Farmer put a collar on me and made me a watchdog.
But in November the watchdog announced people with newly diagnosed, mild Alzheimer's were exempt.
All four have denied breaking parliamentary rules but have referred themselves to the standards watchdog.
